ref: Are you blind? Which is more important, the gift or the altar that makes the gift sacred?
text: Banɛkunɛ mianɛ nuwĩ! Fɔɔdabe bɛ̃ kɔya? Futetsa a mbɔɛɛ kĩ afɔlibɔ atɛnyɛ a kĩ ye lɛkpalɛ futetsa a akũ a?
translated: Hello everyone! How are you all doing? Do you know the story of the brave hunter who killed the lion with a spear?

ref: But Jesus did not answer. So the high priest said, “With the living God looking on, you must tell the truth. Are you the Messiah, the Son of God?”
text: Lɛmɛ Yesu tábinya onukpɛ̃. Ade Ɔsɔfɔnɔkɔɛ̃ Flee a buki ebi ye kĩ, “Ntani Baguma kĩ edzi nkpã a kĩ, bi boe ntɛ nɔ nyɛ Kristo, Baguma Obi a.”
translated: Jesus said to them, "Truly, truly, I say to you, whoever hears my word and believes him who sent me has eternal life. He does not come into judgment, but has passed from death to life."

ref: Jesus asked them how much food they had. They replied, “Seven small loaves of bread and a few little fish.”
text: Yesu via be kĩ, “Abodoo eni minɛɛ?” Bibi ye kĩ, “Abodoo evũkɔnɔ mɔ kɔfɔabi wuluwulu sukuloo deĩ.”
translated: Jesus said to him, “Who is your neighbor?” He replied, “The one who showed mercy to me.”

ref: The people taking care of the pigs ran to the town and told everything, especially what had happened to the two men.
text: Bayaɛ akũ badãmɛnanɛ a lo bɛnaa ɔmatɔ kamɛ bɛnalɛ fɔlɛtsa a kĩ fɔya a flee nɛ banyole nviã a kĩ hũhũ kpa na nɛ be kamɛ a akũ.
translated: The old woman went to the market to buy food for her children, she bought rice, beans, and fish. She returned home and cooked the food.

ref: From Jerusalem and all Judea and from the Jordan River Valley crowds of people went to John.
text: Bati kato Yerusalem, Yudea tite flee mɔ tite a kĩ tifuã Yordan ɔwɔ a flee akũ kaya nɛ ye sɛkɛ̃.
translated: The people of Jerusalem, Judea, fled to the other side of the Jordan River, and they found refuge in the mountains.
